resources.string: No scans yet. Scan a QR code to get started.
Are you sure you want to delete this scan?
Could not read QR code from image
QR code does not contain valid scan data
Camera permission is required to scan QR codes
Indicates the total body weight. Body weight can fluctuate daily and alone does not provide any information about changes in muscle mass and body fat percentage.

There is water in the body both inside and outside the body\'s cells. An adult\'s body is made up of 45%–65% water. The level of the share is influenced by various factors, such as age and gender. Large deviations in the body water between two measurements can influence the measurement results. A constant fluid balance should be provided for optimal comparability.

This value describes the total fat mass in the body. The higher the value, the greater the risk of diseases such as diabetes. If you lose weight, this value should decrease and muscle mass should be maintained.
Skeletal muscles are the muscles that are primarily responsible for voluntary, active body movements, such as the movement of arms and legs.
This value indicates the pure muscle mass without the water content. This value can be increased through intensive strength training and a sufficient intake of nutritional protein.

Excess fatty tissue on the abdomen is referred to as visceral fat. This accumulates around the internal organs and is particularly unhealthy. The more belly fat, the greater the risk of cardiovascular disease and diabetes.
The biological age relates to the physical condition. This can be positively influenced by a healthy lifestyle and reduces biological age.
The basal metabolic rate shows the calorie consumption at rest. The body burns this amount of calories in just 24 hours in order to maintain all functions necessary for survival.

The Health Score is a summary of your measurement. It puts your measurement results in relation and evaluates them using valid standard values that relate to your gender and height. The higher your health score, the healthier your body is according to the general criteria for body water, muscle protein, body fat and bone mineral content.
